## What this PR does

One paragraph. What changes, and why.

## Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature (links to a `[PROPOSAL]` issue: #___)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Benchmark version increment (`vX.Y.Z → vX.Y.(Z+1)`) with DELTA record
- [ ] CI / build / tooling
- [ ] Other:

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass locally.
- [ ] No `.pem`, no `*key*.pem`, no `*private*` files added.
- [ ] Documentation updated where behavior changed.
- [ ] Epistemic markers (VERIFIED / PLAUSIBLE / SPECULATIVE) applied to new claims.
- [ ] If touching the benchmark: linked DELTA record drafted; manifest hash recomputed; OTS plan documented.
- [ ] If touching cryptography or chain handling: round-trip test included.
- [ ] Commit message follows convention: `<area>: <imperative summary>` (see CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Failure mode

How could this PR be wrong? What would surface that? This is not a request for excessive humility — it is the same discipline applied to PR descriptions that the project applies to benchmark proposals.
